Umple -- Model-Oriented Programming

Add UML abstractions like associations, attributes, and state machines directly into Java, Python, C++, and PHP. Write less code. Ship reliable software.

Try Online Download

What is Umple?

Umple is a modeling tool and programming language family that enables Model-Oriented Programming. It adds abstractions such as associations, attributes, and state machines to object-oriented programming languages such as Java, Python, C++ and PHP. Umple can also be used to create many kinds of software engineering diagrams textually.

Umple creates reliable code from highly-readable abstractions. The resulting system has many fewer lines than if programmed in a typical programming language, since developers avoid writing boilerplate code. Unlike AI-generated code, the boilerplate never has to be inspected or maintained, and has been tested so it is reliable.

Umple is an open source project hosted on GitHub. The Umple compiler itself is written in Umple. Any Java, Python, C++ or PHP project can be adapted to use Umple.

Example

class Student {}

class CourseSection {}

class Registration
{
  String grade;
  * -- 1 Student;
  * -- 1 CourseSection;
}

Declare attributes and associations in a few lines. Load this in UmpleOnline

Key Features

🔗

Associations

Define relationships between classes directly in code. Umple generates all the boilerplate for adding, removing, and maintaining referential integrity.

🔄

State Machines

Model complex behavior with state machines as first-class language constructs. Visualize them as diagrams or edit them graphically.

⚙️

Multi-Language Code Generation

Generate production-ready code in Java, Python, C++, or PHP from a single Umple model. The generated code is reliable and never needs manual maintenance.

📊

UML Diagrams

Create class diagrams, state diagrams, and other software engineering diagrams textually. Umple renders them automatically.

🌐

Browser-Based Editor

Use UmpleOnline to model, generate code, and visualize diagrams right in your browser. No installation required.

🤖

AI Integration

Combine Umple with AI code generation: ask your AI to generate data models or state models in Umple directly from requirements.

Get Started

▶ Try Online Model and generate code in your browser ⬇ Download Command line, VS Code, Eclipse plugins 📖 Documentation Complete user manual with live examples 💻 GitHub Source code, issues, and contributions

Recent Advances

  • 2026: New versions of plugins for VSCode, and new plugin for the Zed editor
  • 2026: A language server protocol (LSP) server repository powers various IDEs
  • 2026: Umple skills repository to help AIs use Umple
  • 2026: CRUD forms generation tool with load and save capability
  • 2026: Dark mode and filtering in UmpleOnline
  • 2026: Multiple class diagram layout algorithms
  • 2025: Instance diagram generation
  • 2025: Collaborative editing
  • 2024: Editable state machine diagrams
  • 2024: Requirements embedding with req and implementsReq keywords
  • 2024: JSON generation and loading with genJson suboption
  • 2024: Python code generation

See the release notes for full details.

Umple is developed at the University of Ottawa with support from NSERC and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.
